notes.note: Renders the Roman legal formula nuntium remittere, the formal declaration that dissolved a marriage; Cicero is weighing whether Tullia should send it to her husband Dolabella amid the political turmoil of 47 BC.

notes.anchor: what that fellow's power is at the moment
notes.note: The unnamed 'that fellow' is Tullia's husband P. Cornelius Dolabella, then a turbulent tribune-elect / Caesarian agitator at Rome; Cicero leaves him pointedly anonymous because the family was debating divorcing him.
